title: పది కత్తులు పట్టిన యోధుడు
moral: నిజమైన బలం అంటే — కండలు తిరిగిన శరీరంలో ఉండదు. మనలోని ప్రత్యేక నైపుణ్యాన్ని, ఇతరులకు సహాయం చేయడంలోనే అది దాగి ఉంటుంది.
main_character: Ramu, a lanky teenage boy with long, nimble fingers stained with wood sap and a perpetually dusty tunic. He is incredibly skilled at carving but clumsy in everything else, often tripping over his own shadow. Always the same character across every scene.
setting: The sun-drenched village of Mangapuram in the Rayalaseema region, nestled by a sprawling ancient banyan tree and endless mango groves. The air is thick with the scent of ripe mangoes and jasmine, and the story unfolds from dawn to the next dawn. Same location across every scene.

scenes.text: మామిడి వాసనతో నిండిన మంగాపురం గ్రామంలో — రాముడు చెక్క పనిలో లీనమై ఉన్నాడు. అతని చేతుల్లోని వేప కర్ర నుండి తీపి, పదునైన వాసన గుప్పుమంది. ఒక అందమైన చిలుక బొమ్మ ప్రాణం పోసుకుంది. చిన్నారి చేతికి అది అందిస్తూ — రాముడు మురిసిపోయాడు. ఇంతలో, ఒక్కసారిగా ఒక వేరుకు తగిలి కిందపడ్డాడు. తల రుద్దుకుంటూ, సిగ్గుతో నవ్వాడు. "అయ్యో! ఈ కాలు ఎప్పుడూ నా మాట వినదు!" అని గొణిగాడు. ఇంత అనామకుడైన రాముడు — ఒక వీరుడు ఎలా అవుతాడు?
scenes.image_prompt: Ramu skillfully carves a beautiful wooden parrot for a small child, then promptly trips over a root while handing it to her. Character feels proud then embarrassed — smiling at his creation, then blushing and rubbing his head after falling. the sweet, sharp scent of freshly carved neem wood. Ramu, a lanky teenage boy with long, nimble fingers stained with wood sap and a perpetually dusty tunic. He is incredibly skilled at carving but clumsy in everything else, often tripping over his own shadow. Always the same character across every scene. The sun-drenched village of Mangapuram in the Rayalaseema region, nestled by a sprawling ancient banyan tree and endless mango groves. The air is thick with the scent of ripe mangoes and jasmine, and the story unfolds from dawn to the next dawn. Same location across every scene. Soft golden afternoon sunlight, warm hopeful mood. Hand-painted children's storybook illustration, classic Indian Chandamama and Amar Chitra Katha style, soft watercolor textures, rounded friendly shapes, warm earth-tone palette (terracotta, cream, sage green, warm ochre), clean composition with breathing space. NOT photorealistic. NOT 3D render. NOT anime. NOT dark or gritty. NO cracked earth, NO apocalyptic atmosphere, NO aggressive poses, NO spread wings unless scene is explicitly about flying, NO menacing expressions. Characters should look gentle, friendly, and age-appropriate for children 5-10.
scenes.text_te_en: Maamidi Vaasanato Nindina Mangaapuran Graamanlo — Raamudu Chekka Panilo Linamai Unnaadu. Atani Chetulloni Vepa Karra Nundi Tipi, Padunaina Vaasana Guppumandi. Oka Andamaina Chiluka Bomma Praanan Posukundi. Chinnaari Chetiki Adi Andistu — Raamudu Murisipoyaadu. Intalo, Okkasaarigaa Oka Veruku Tagili Kindapaddaadu. Tala Ruddukuntu, Sigguto Navvaadu. "Ayyo! I Kaalu Eppudu Naa Maata Vinadu!" Ani Gonigaadu. Inta Anaamakudaina Raamudu — Oka Virudu Elaa Avutaadu?
scenes.text_en: In Mangapuram village, where the air smelled of sweet mangoes, Ramu was busy carving wood. A sweet, sharp scent came from the neem wood in his hands. A beautiful parrot toy seemed to come alive! Ramu smiled happily as he gave it to a little child. Just then, he tripped over a root and tumbled down. Rubbing his head, he gave a shy laugh. "Oh dear! This leg never listens to me!" he grumbled. How could an ordinary Ramu like this ever become a hero?

scenes.text: మరుసటి రోజు ఉదయం — గ్రామ పెద్ద ఒక భయంకరమైన వార్త చెప్పాడు. షాడో సోర్సరర్ శాపం వారి మామిడి తోటపై పడిందట. గ్రామస్తుల గుసగుసలు — చెదిరిన తేనెటీగల గుంపులా వినిపించాయి. "పది పనులు ఒకేసారి పూర్తి చేస్తేనే ఆ మాయగాడి శాపం పోతుంది!" అని పెద్దాయన గంభీరంగా ప్రకటించాడు. రాముడు తన చెక్కుడు కత్తిని గట్టిగా పట్టుకున్నాడు — నుదురు ముడిపడింది. పిల్లలూ, ఆ పది పనులు ఒకేసారి ఎవరు చేయగలరు?
scenes.image_prompt: The village elder announces that a curse from the Shadow Sorcerer has fallen upon their mango grove, which can only be broken by performing ten specific tasks simultaneously before the next sunrise. Character feels worried — listening intently with a furrowed brow, his hands clutching his carving knife. the worried murmur of the villagers, like a hive of disturbed bees. Ramu, a lanky teenage boy with long, nimble fingers stained with wood sap and a perpetually dusty tunic. He is incredibly skilled at carving but clumsy in everything else, often tripping over his own shadow. Always the same character across every scene. The sun-drenched village of Mangapuram in the Rayalaseema region, nestled by a sprawling ancient banyan tree and endless mango groves. The air is thick with the scent of ripe mangoes and jasmine, and the story unfolds from dawn to the next dawn. Same location across every scene. Diffused daylight, gentle curious mood (NOT dark, NOT stormy). Hand-painted children's storybook illustration, classic Indian Chandamama and Amar Chitra Katha style, soft watercolor textures, rounded friendly shapes, warm earth-tone palette (terracotta, cream, sage green, warm ochre), clean composition with breathing space. NOT photorealistic. NOT 3D render. NOT anime. NOT dark or gritty. NO cracked earth, NO apocalyptic atmosphere, NO aggressive poses, NO spread wings unless scene is explicitly about flying, NO menacing expressions. Characters should look gentle, friendly, and age-appropriate for children 5-10.
scenes.text_te_en: Marusati Roju Udayan — Graama Pedda Oka Bhayankaramaina Vaarta Cheppaadu. Shaado Sorsarar Shaapan Vaari Maamidi Totapai Padindata. Graamastula Gusagusalu — Chedirina Tenetigala Gumpulaa Vinipinchaayi. "Padi Panulu Okesaari Purti Chestene Aa Maayagaadi Shaapan Potundi!" Ani Peddaayana Gambhirangaa Prakatinchaadu. Raamudu Tana Chekkudu Kattini Gattigaa Pattukunnaadu — Nuduru Mudipadindi. Pillalu, Aa Padi Panulu Okesaari Evaru Cheyagalaru?
scenes.text_en: The next morning, the village elder shared some terrible news. The Shadow Sorcerer's curse had fallen upon their mango orchard! The villagers' whispers buzzed like a swarm of scattered bees. "Only if ten tasks are completed at once will the sorcerer's curse be broken!" the elder solemnly announced. Ramu gripped his carving knife tightly, his forehead wrinkled in thought. Children, who could possibly do ten tasks all at the same time?

scenes.text: గ్రామంలోని బలమైన మనుషులు పది పనులు ఒకేసారి చేయాలని ప్రయత్నించారు. కానీ అంతా గందరగోళంగా మారింది — ఒకరికొకరు అడ్డు తగిలారు, వాదించుకున్నారు. రాముడు మర్రిచెట్టు కింద కూర్చున్నాడు, `చెట్టు బెరడు` అతని వీపుకు హాయిగా ఉంది. కోపంగా ఒక కర్ర ముక్కను చెక్కుతున్నాడు, చిన్న చిన్న shavings గాలిలో ఎగురుతున్నాయి. "నా చేతుల్లో నైపుణ్యం ఉంది, కానీ నాలో బలం లేదు." అని నిట్టూర్చాడు. తన చేతి నైపుణ్యం — ఇప్పుడు ఏం చేయగలదు?
scenes.image_prompt: Ramu watches helplessly as the village's strongest men try to coordinate the ten tasks but fail chaotically, arguing and getting in each other's way. Character feels frustrated and useless — sitting under the banyan tree, angrily carving shavings off a piece of wood. the rough, comforting bark of the banyan tree against his back. Ramu, a lanky teenage boy with long, nimble fingers stained with wood sap and a perpetually dusty tunic. He is incredibly skilled at carving but clumsy in everything else, often tripping over his own shadow. Always the same character across every scene. The sun-drenched village of Mangapuram in the Rayalaseema region, nestled by a sprawling ancient banyan tree and endless mango groves. The air is thick with the scent of ripe mangoes and jasmine, and the story unfolds from dawn to the next dawn. Same location across every scene. Diffused daylight, gentle curious mood (NOT dark, NOT stormy). NOT photorealistic. NOT 3D render. NOT anime. NOT dark or gritty. NO cracked earth, NO apocalyptic atmosphere, NO aggressive poses, NO spread wings unless scene is explicitly about flying, NO menacing expressions. Characters should look gentle, friendly, and age-appropriate for children 5-10.
scenes.text_te_en: Graamanloni Balamaina Manushulu Padi Panulu Okesaari Cheyaalani Prayatninchaaru. Kaani Antaa Gandaragolangaa Maarindi — Okarikokaru Addu Tagilaaru, Vaadinchukunnaaru. Raamudu Marrichettu Kinda Kurchunnaadu, `Chettu Beradu` Atani Vipuku Haayigaa Undi. Kopangaa Oka Karra Mukkanu Chekkutunnaadu, Chinna Chinna Shavings Gaalilo Egurutunnaayi. "Naa Chetullo Naipunyan Undi, Kaani Naalo Balan Ledu." Ani Nitturchaadu. Tana Cheti Naipunyan — Ippudu En Cheyagaladu?
scenes.text_en: The strongest men in the village tried to do ten tasks at once. But it was all a big mess! They bumped into each other and argued. Ramu sat under a banyan tree, the rough bark feeling nice against his back. He angrily carved a piece of wood, sending tiny shavings flying into the air. "I have skill in my hands, but no strength," he sighed. What could his special carving skill do now?

scenes.text: ఇంతలో, రాముడికి ఒక్కసారిగా ఒక అద్భుతమైన ఆలోచన తట్టింది. అతని కళ్ళు పెద్దవయ్యాయి, చేతులు మెరుపులా కదిలాయి. అతను తనలాగే పది చిన్న చెక్క బొమ్మలు చెక్కాడు — ప్రతి బొమ్మ చేతిలో ఒక పరికరం: ఒక చిన్న కొడవలి, ఒక నీరు పోసే డబ్బా. "బలంతో కాదు, ఉపాయంతో ప్రయత్నిస్తాను!" అని గుసగుసలాడాడు. `పది చిన్న, ఒకేలాంటి చెక్క ముఖాలు` — నిశ్చయమైన చూపులతో అతని వంక చూస్తున్నాయి. కానీ ఈ నిర్జీవ బొమ్మలు నిజమైన పనిని ఎలా చేస్తాయి?
scenes.image_prompt: An idea strikes Ramu, and he rapidly carves ten small wooden figures of himself, each holding a different tool: a tiny sickle, a watering can, a broom, and so on. Character feels hopeful and focused — his eyes wide with inspiration, his hands moving like a blur. the sight of ten tiny, identical wooden faces with determined expressions staring up at him. Ramu, a lanky teenage boy with long, nimble fingers stained with wood sap and a perpetually dusty tunic. He is incredibly skilled at carving but clumsy in everything else, often tripping over his own shadow. Always the same character across every scene. The sun-drenched village of Mangapuram in the Rayalaseema region, nestled by a sprawling ancient banyan tree and endless mango groves. The air is thick with the scent of ripe mangoes and jasmine, and the story unfolds from dawn to the next dawn. Same location across every scene. Dappled shade with quiet contemplative light. Characters should look gentle, friendly, and age-appropriate for children 5-10.
scenes.text_te_en: Intalo, Raamudiki Okkasaarigaa Oka Adbhutamaina Aalochana Tattindi. Atani Kallu Peddavayyaayi, Chetulu Merupulaa Kadilaayi. Atanu Tanalaage Padi Chinna Chekka Bommalu Chekkaadu — Prati Bomma Chetilo Oka Parikaran: Oka Chinna Kodavali, Oka Niru Pose Dabbaa. "Balanto Kaadu, Upaayanto Prayatnistaanu!" Ani Gusagusalaadaadu. `Padi Chinna, Okelaanti Chekka Mukhaalu` — Nishchayamaina Chupulato Atani Vanka Chustunnaayi. Kaani I Nirjiva Bommalu Nijamaina Panini Elaa Chestaayi?
scenes.text_en: Just then, a wonderful idea popped into Ramu's head! His eyes grew wide, and his hands moved super fast. He carved ten small wooden figures, just like himself. Each little figure held a tiny tool: a small sickle, a watering can. "Not with strength, but with a clever plan!" he whispered. Ten small, identical wooden faces looked back at him with determined eyes. But how could these lifeless toys do real work?

scenes.text: చీకటి పడింది, రాముడు నిద్రపోలేదు. తన అమ్మమ్మ చెప్పిన ఒక ప్రాచీన లాలిపాట అతనికి గుర్తుకు వచ్చింది. ఆ పాట కర్రకు ప్రాణం పోస్తుందని ఆమె చెప్పింది. బొమ్మలను చేతుల్లో పట్టుకుని — మెల్లగా, కళ్ళు మూసుకుని పాడాడు. అతని ఆశ అంతా ఆ పాటలో నిండి ఉంది. `లాలిపాట సన్నగా గుసగుసలాడుతోంది`, బొమ్మల గుండా అది కంపించింది. "అమ్మమ్మ, మీ పాట ఈ కర్రకు ప్రాణం పోస్తుందని నమ్ముతున్నాను." అని మనసులో వేడుకున్నాడు. సూర్యోదయానికి ముందు — ఈ పాట నిజంగా మాయ చేస్తుందా?
scenes.image_prompt: Remembering his grandmother's tales, Ramu stays up all night under the moonlight, singing an ancient lullaby that is said to breathe life into the heart of the wood. Character feels desperate yet determined — singing softly with his eyes closed, pouring all his hope into the melody. the low, humming melody of the lullaby vibrating through the wooden figures in his hands. Ramu, a lanky teenage boy with long, nimble fingers stained with wood sap and a perpetually dusty tunic. He is incredibly skilled at carving but clumsy in everything else, often tripping over his own shadow. Always the same character across every scene. The sun-drenched village of Mangapuram in the Rayalaseema region, nestled by a sprawling ancient banyan tree and endless mango groves. The air is thick with the scent of ripe mangoes and jasmine, and the story unfolds from dawn to the next dawn. Same location across every scene. Dappled shade with quiet contemplative light. NOT photorealistic. NOT 3D render. NOT anime. NOT dark or gritty. NO cracked earth, NO apocalyptic atmosphere, NO aggressive poses, NO spread wings unless scene is explicitly about flying, NO menacing expressions. Characters should look gentle, friendly, and age-appropriate for children 5-10.
scenes.text_te_en: Chikati Padindi, Raamudu Nidrapoledu. Tana Ammamma Cheppina Oka Praachina Laalipaata Ataniki Gurtuku Vachchindi. Aa Paata Karraku Praanan Postundani Aame Cheppindi. Bommalanu Chetullo Pattukuni — Mellagaa, Kallu Musukuni Paadaadu. Atani Aasha Antaa Aa Paatalo Nindi Undi. `Laalipaata Sannagaa Gusagusalaadutondi`, Bommala Gundaa Adi Kampinchindi. "Ammamma, Mi Paata I Karraku Praanan Postundani Nammutunnaanu." Ani Manasulo Vedukunnaadu. Suryodayaaniki Mundu — I Paata Nijangaa Maaya Chestundaa?
scenes.text_en: Darkness fell, but Ramu couldn't sleep. He remembered an old lullaby his grandma used to sing. She had told him that the song could bring wood to life. Holding the little figures in his hands, he sang softly, with his eyes closed. All his hope was in that song. The lullaby whispered gently, and it seemed to hum through the wooden figures. "Grandma, I believe your song can bring this wood to life," he wished in his heart. Before the sun came up, would this song really work its magic?

scenes.text: పొద్దుపొడిచే సమయం దగ్గరపడింది. మొదటి సూర్యకిరణం ఆ చెక్క బొమ్మలపై పడింది... ఒక్కసారిగా అవి జీవం పోసుకున్నాయి! `పది చిన్న చెక్క అడుగుల చప్పుడు` — పొడి నేలపై గిర్రున పరిగెత్తాయి. అవి ఒకేసారి, ఒకే సమన్వయంతో పది పనులకు బయలుదేరాయి. రాముడి కళ్ళల్లో ఆనంద బాష్పాలు. "అవి... అవి కదులుతున్నాయి! నా యోధులు!" అని కేక పెట్టాడు. ఆ బొమ్మలు అన్ని పనులు సూర్యోదయం లోపల పూర్తవుతాయా?
scenes.image_prompt: As the first ray of dawn strikes the figures, they spring to life, and the ten tiny Ramu-warriors scurry off in perfect coordination to perform the ten tasks across the village. Character feels astonished and overjoyed — watching with wide, tear-filled eyes as his creations come alive. the crisp, clattering sound of ten little wooden feet running on dry earth. Ramu, a lanky teenage boy with long, nimble fingers stained with wood sap and a perpetually dusty tunic. He is incredibly skilled at carving but clumsy in everything else, often tripping over his own shadow. Always the same character across every scene. The sun-drenched village of Mangapuram in the Rayalaseema region, nestled by a sprawling ancient banyan tree and endless mango groves. The air is thick with the scent of ripe mangoes and jasmine, and the story unfolds from dawn to the next dawn. Same location across every scene. Bright clear daylight, playful energetic mood. Hand-painted children's storybook illustration, classic Indian Chandamama and Amar Chitra Katha style, soft watercolor textures, rounded friendly shapes, warm earth-tone palette (terracotta, cream, sage green, warm ochre), clean composition with breathing space. NOT photorealistic. NOT 3D render. NOT anime. NOT dark or gritty. NO cracked earth, NO apocalyptic atmosphere, NO aggressive poses, NO spread wings unless scene is explicitly about flying, NO menacing expressions. Characters should look gentle, friendly, and age-appropriate for children 5-10.
scenes.text_te_en: Poddupodiche Samayan Daggarapadindi. Modati Suryakiranan Aa Chekka Bommalapai Padindi... Okkasaarigaa Avi Jivan Posukunnaayi! `Padi Chinna Chekka Adugula Chappudu` — Podi Nelapai Girruna Parigettaayi. Avi Okesaari, Oke Samanvayanto Padi Panulaku Bayaluderaayi. Raamudi Kallallo Aananda Baashpaalu. "Avi... Avi Kadulutunnaayi! Naa Yodhulu!" Ani Keka Pettaadu. Aa Bommalu Anni Panulu Suryodayan Lopala Purtavutaayaa?
scenes.text_en: Sunrise was almost here. The first ray of sun touched the wooden figures... and suddenly they came to life! The sound of ten tiny wooden footsteps scurried across the dry ground. They set off for the ten tasks all at once, working together perfectly. Tears of joy filled Ramu's eyes. "They... they're moving! My warriors!" he cried out. Would the figures finish all the tasks before the sun fully rose?

scenes.text: సూర్యుడు పూర్తిగా ఉదయించాడు. శాపం ఒక్కసారిగా వెలుగు మెరుపుతో విరిగిపోయింది — తోటంతా `వెచ్చని బంగారు కాంతితో` నిండిపోయింది. `వర్షం పడిన నేల, మామిడి వాసన` గాలిలో కలగలిసిపోయింది. పది చిన్న చెక్క యోధులు — సగర్వంగా, పనులు పూర్తి చేసి నిలబడి ఉన్నాయి. పిల్లలు ఆ చిన్న బొమ్మల చుట్టూ గుమిగూడారు. "అన్నా, నువ్వే మా పది కత్తులు పట్టిన యోధుడు!" అని ఒక చిన్నారి కేక వేసింది. రాముడు శాంతంగా నవ్వాడు — తన పని పూర్తయింది.
scenes.image_prompt: Just as the sun fully rises, the curse breaks with a shimmer of light, and the mango grove is bathed in a healthy golden glow, with the ten tiny wooden figures standing proudly together. Character feels peaceful and content — smiling quietly as children gather around his little heroes, not needing any praise for himself. the warm, golden sunlight on his face, smelling of rain-washed earth and sweet mangoes. Ramu, a lanky teenage boy with long, nimble fingers stained with wood sap and a perpetually dusty tunic. He is incredibly skilled at carving but clumsy in everything else, often tripping over his own shadow. Always the same character across every scene. The sun-drenched village of Mangapuram in the Rayalaseema region, nestled by a sprawling ancient banyan tree and endless mango groves. The air is thick with the scent of ripe mangoes and jasmine, and the story unfolds from dawn to the next dawn. Same location across every scene. Warm golden-hour glow, triumphant peaceful mood. Hand-painted children's storybook illustration, classic Indian Chandamama and Amar Chitra Katha style, soft watercolor textures, rounded friendly shapes, warm earth-tone palette (terracotta, cream, sage green, warm ochre), clean composition with breathing space. NOT photorealistic. NOT 3D render. NOT anime. NOT dark or gritty. NO cracked earth, NO apocalyptic atmosphere, NO aggressive poses, NO spread wings unless scene is explicitly about flying, NO menacing expressions. Characters should look gentle, friendly, and age-appropriate for children 5-10.
scenes.text_te_en: Suryudu Purtigaa Udayinchaadu. Shaapan Okkasaarigaa Velugu Meruputo Virigipoyindi — Totantaa `Vechchani Bangaaru Kaantito` Nindipoyindi. `Varshan Padina Nela, Maamidi Vaasana` Gaalilo Kalagalisipoyindi. Padi Chinna Chekka Yodhulu — Sagarvangaa, Panulu Purti Chesi Nilabadi Unnaayi. Pillalu Aa Chinna Bommala Chuttu Gumigudaaru. "Annaa, Nuvve Maa Padi Kattulu Pattina Yodhudu!" Ani Oka Chinnaari Keka Vesindi. Raamudu Shaantangaa Navvaadu — Tana Pani Purtayindi.
scenes.text_en: The sun had fully risen. The curse broke with a flash of light – the whole orchard was filled with a warm, golden glow. The smell of rain-soaked earth and sweet mangoes filled the air. The ten small wooden warriors stood proudly, their tasks all done. Children gathered around the little figures. "Brother, you are our warrior of ten swords!" a little child cheered. Ramu smiled calmly – his work was complete.

quality_scores.notes: The story's structure is exceptional, particularly its use of scene-ending hooks which makes it incredibly engaging for a child. The narrative arc, showing a hero succeeding through cleverness rather than brute force, is classic and well-executed. The primary weakness is a slight inconsistency in the narrative voice, which sometimes shifts from a warm, oral tone to a more formal, written one. To reach a publishable 9+ score, the single most important fix is to make the narrator's voice consistently feel like a loving grandparent and replace the English word 'shavings' with an authentic Telugu term like 'చెక్క పొట్టు' to maintain perfect immersion.

topic: తెలుగు జానపద కథ - పది కత్తులు పట్టిన యోధుడు
moral_te_en: Nijamaina Balan Ante — Kandalu Tirigina Shariranlo Undadu. Manaloni Pratyeka Naipunyaanni, Itarulaku Sahaayan Cheyadanlone Adi Daagi Untundi.
moral_en: True strength isn't found in big, strong muscles. It's hidden in our special skills and in helping others.
